title: 「It is + 形容詞 +（for + 人）+ to + 動詞」句型
titleZh: 「It is + 形容詞 +（for + 人）+ to + 動詞」句
titleEn: Using It + adjective + (for someone) + to + verb

source.file: Sentence Structure 35 「It is + 形容詞 +（for + 人）+ to + 動詞」句.pdf
omissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are faithful editorial translations.

formula: It + be + adjective + to + base verb + other information

formulas.labelEn: A. Describing a general characteristic of an action
formulas.labelZh: A. 說明一件事的一般特點
formulas.formula: It + be + adjective + to + base verb + other information

formulas.labelEn: B. Describing the situation for a particular person
formulas.labelZh: B. 說明某人做一件事時的情況
formulas.formula: It + be + adjective + for + person / object pronoun + to + base verb + other information

formulas.labelZh: C. 對一個說法作出評價
formulas.formula: It + be + adjective + to say + (that) + clause

example: It is important to read the instructions carefully.

examples.en: It is important to read the instructions carefully.
It was hard for me to describe the experience.
examples.zh: 對我來說，要形容那段經歷很困難。
examples.highlight: It was hard for me to describe

examples.en: It is sad to say that we cannot finish the task before the deadline.
examples.zh: 很遺憾地說，我們無法在截止日期前完成任務。
examples.highlight: It is sad to say that

examples.en: It is safe to say that the project will go as scheduled.
examples.zh: 可以肯定地說，這個項目將會按計劃進行。
examples.highlight: It is safe to say that

meaning.zh: 以形式主語 It 先提出評價，再用 to + 動詞 說明真正的行動；也可加入 for + 人 指明該評價適用於誰。

rules.en: It normally does not refer to an actual thing. It is a dummy subject, while the real action follows to + verb.
rules.zh: It 通常不代表任何實際事物，而是形式主語；真正要談論的行動放在 to + 動詞 後面。
examples: Learning a new language is difficult.
It is difficult to learn a new language.

rules.en: Use for + person + to + verb when specifying who performs the action.
rules.zh: 指明做動作的人時，在形容詞後加入 for + 人 + to + 動詞。
examples: It is difficult for Leo to lift the box.

rules.en: Use an object pronoun after for: me, you, him, her, us or them.
rules.zh: For 後面要使用受詞形式，例如 me、you、him、her、us 或 them。
examples: It is difficult for him to understand the question.

rules.en: Omit for + person when the meaning applies to people in general.
rules.zh: 如果意思適用於一般人，可以省略 for + 人。
examples: It is dangerous to touch a wet electrical plug.

rules.en: Use the base form of the verb after to.
rules.zh: To 後面使用動詞原形。
examples: It is important to check the answer.

rules.en: Change be to match tense or modality: is, was, will be, may be, can be or would be.
rules.zh: Be 可以按照時態或語氣改變，例如 is、was、will be、may be、can be 或 would be。

rules.en: For and of have different meanings. This lesson uses for to evaluate an action for a person; of usually comments on a person’s character or behaviour.
rules.zh: For 和 of 的意思不同。本課使用 for，表示某人做某件事時該行動的情況；of + 人 通常評論一個人的性格或行為，屬於另一個句型。
examples: It is difficult for Ben to solve the problem.
It is kind of Ben to help us.

benefits.en: It lets readers know your evaluation first.
benefits.zh: 把 important、difficult、safe、dangerous 等重點放在句子前面，讓讀者立即知道你的評價。

benefits.en: It clearly identifies who experiences the situation.
benefits.zh: 加入 for + 人 後，可以準確指出某件事對誰來說容易、困難、重要或有可能。

benefits.en: It makes longer sentences easier to read.
benefits.zh: 可以先給出簡短評價，再把較長的行動放在後面，使句子較容易跟隨。

benefits.en: It is suitable for advice, reminders and warnings.
benefits.zh: 可以自然地告訴別人甚麼事情是重要、必要、明智或危險的。

benefits.en: It helps you make a cautious evaluation.
benefits.zh: It is safe to say that... 和 It is fair to say that... 可用來提出有理據、但不過分絕對的判斷。

benefits.en: It provides an easy speaking frame.
benefits.zh: 先使用 It is + adjective...，再加入自己的內容，可減少說話時從零開始組織整句的壓力。

sourceOmissions: The PDF presents the Important Rules and Benefits explanations mainly in Chinese; their English versions are faithful editorial translations.

en: Rewrite each sentence using It + be + adjective + (for + person) + to + verb.
Use a suitable form of be and preserve the original meaning.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 It + be + 形容詞 +（for + 人）+ to + 動詞 改寫以下句子。
請使用適當的 be 形式，並保留原句意思。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: Making a cup of tea with this machine is easy.
questions.promptZh: 使用這部機器泡一杯茶很容易。
questions.answer: It is easy to make a cup of tea with this machine.
questions.answerZh: 使用這部機器泡一杯茶很容易。
questions.highlight: It is easy to make a cup of tea with this machine.

questions.prompt: Washing your hands before eating is important.
questions.promptZh: 飯前洗手很重要。
questions.answer: It is important to wash your hands before eating.
questions.answerZh: 飯前洗手很重要。
questions.highlight: It is important to wash your hands before eating.

questions.prompt: Leo has difficulty lifting this heavy box.
questions.promptZh: 里奧搬起這個沉重的箱子有困難。
questions.answer: It is difficult for Leo to lift this heavy box.

questions.answer: It was embarrassing for Tom to forget his teacher’s name.

questions.prompt: We are encouraged by the fact that more young people are volunteering in the community.
